$comment: Machine-readable map of SPEC.md / runner-contract.md behaviors to the conformance fixture(s) that gate them. "automated" entries are asserted by `npm run conformance:run` (the suite-runner drives the harness and asserts captured mock traffic). "manual" entries are NOT covered by the 30 fixtures and must be verified out-of-band; they include BOTH SHOULD-level behaviors (e.g. time/idle flush) AND MUST-level requirements that need a controllable clock, a connection-drop mock, or a non-fixture input (e.g. the prod fail-closed mock-endpoint gate, constructor validation, process-wide logging state, typed 0.0->float). See conformance/README.md.

automated.behavior: Schema extraction: primitive classification (boolean/int/string/float)
null and undefined classify as 'null'
Empty/falsy values: false->boolean, 0->int, ''->string, {}->object children [], []->list(string) children []
Nested object: recursive children of own properties
List of primitives: children is deduplicated type strings
Heterogeneous list: propertyType from first element; children = mapping() per element after removeDuplicates
Null top-level eventProperties returns []
List deduplication by value (primitives) collapses repeated types
Full wire body shape + required base fields on every event
Format-validated fields: messageId (UUID v4 lowercase), createdAt (ISO-8601 ms), libVersion (SemVer), libPlatform (non-empty)
Sampling drop at samplingRate=0.0 -> no enqueue, zero HTTP calls
samplingRate=1.0 boundary -> event always sent (exactly 1 HTTP call)
Non-200 HTTP response -> promise resolves (not rejects); immediate-send (batchSize==1) resolve value is []
streamId with ':' passed through verbatim (warned, not fatal)
Empty/absent streamId -> wire streamId is ''
gzip compression of bodies >= 1024 bytes (Content-Encoding: gzip); mock gunzips and the decompressed body matches
Small body (< 1024 bytes) sent uncompressed; no Content-Encoding header
env-gated mock endpoint honored for dev/staging (fail-closed for prod); AVO_INSPECTOR_MOCK_ENDPOINT used as-is
automated.$note: All fixtures construct env dev/staging, so the override is honored. The prod fail-closed branch is a security MUST verified by SDK unit tests, not by a fixture (no prod fixture exists).

automated.behavior: Batching: single event with batchSize > 1 is buffered, not sent before flush (zero HTTP calls)
Size-trigger flush at batchSize; a batch MAY mix streamId/eventName; trailing flush() drains remainder
flush() force-sends a partial batch below batchSize and resolves
destroy() discards the pending batch unsent (zero HTTP calls)
maxQueueSize overflow drops the OLDEST buffered event (FIFO)
At-most-once: non-200 response does NOT re-queue; second batch is only the new events
Concurrent enqueue + flush atomic swap-and-clear: K concurrent tracks yield exactly K distinct events, all messageIds present and unique
automated.$note: Single-threaded Node exercises concurrent scheduling, not true parallelism (honest limitation per runner-contract); the union assertion is interleaving-invariant.

automated.behavior: Unordered-multiset batch matching: expected batches matched by contents, arrival order NOT asserted
mock_response:null -> mock still started, SDK pointed at it, zero requests asserted (no escape to real endpoint)

manual.behavior: Time / idle scheduled flush after batchFlushSeconds (SHOULD)
manual.reason: Needs a controllable clock; not deterministic in a single short-lived process. Verify via the SDK's own integration tests.

manual.behavior: Transient send failure (network error / timeout) drops the batch with no re-queue (at-most-once)
manual.reason: Needs a mock that simulates a dropped connection / hang past the 10s timeout. batch-5 covers the non-200 no-requeue path automatically; the transient-failure path remains manual.
Not exercised by fixtures (max nesting tested is 3 levels); language-dependent stack-limit behavior.

manual.behavior: 0.0 (whole-valued float) -> 'float' in statically-typed SDKs (MUST); 'int' accepted for JS/TS (reference parser)
manual.reason: Universal fixtures intentionally avoid 0.0 because the JS reference parser emits 'int'. Statically-typed SDKs verify 0.0->float via their own typed test inputs.

manual.behavior: Constructor validation throws on empty/whitespace apiKey or version; invalid env falls back to dev (no throw)
manual.reason: No fixture constructs an invalid instance (all 30 use valid constructor blocks). Verify via the SDK's own unit tests; the harness surfaces a constructor throw as passed:false + exit code 1.
No prod fixture exists (a prod instance would send to api.avo.app). Verify via the SDK's own unit tests; this is a security MUST.

manual.behavior: process-wide shouldLog flag shared across instances; apiKey/body redaction in logs
manual.reason: Logging is observed on stderr, not asserted by the fixtures. Verify via the SDK's own unit tests.
